Helping us seal the rifts to Nayos is the most important thing you can be doing to help Amnytas face the threats of this war. You are providing hope to us, Wayfinder. More than I think you realize.Strength is more than who can conjure the greatest fireball or topple the biggest monster... Strength is our bond. The Kryptis would like to see that bond shattered.Here, take this as a symbol of my appreciation. Think of it as a symbol of our bond.Keep working on your spellcraft,Vanak

Here there be Dragons.
Here! Be careful with it—they're hand carved and very old. Remember, a weakened animal is susceptible to its music. A strong one will resist.

Here's a new one: Careless Whisper reckons the Stranger she spied in the woods last week was "long as a four journeykin" and "tall as a lodge."     She's a fibber, make no mistake (remember what she said two harvests back about your old paw's cabbages?), but the council thinks it's still worth a good poke around the place. Claw's got us chasing shadows, with the wolves gone funny and thick air rolling in. Won't be back down for another moon at least; you tell the cubs their Maw-Maw's coming home with an extra batch of sticky honey cakes for bearkin who behave themselves.      Any sac-fry in the lake yet?     Miss you.     —Dancing Minstrel

Hero! I marvel at your amazing feats. I thank thee for bestowing such bounty upon myself, and my passel of performers. The skies shall hear of your tales of triumph; you have my word. Feel free to visit our village and stay for a show. I promise the performance will be praiseworthy.—Castaway Vice Chief Hernandez
Hero's Chest[s]
Hero's Overlook is home to the ghosts of Crystal Desert's past, back when Ascension was possible.

Hero, there is a disturbance in the Mists. A memory of a battle long past has resurfaced and begins to reinforce its reality.Journey to the Eye of the North and utilize the scrying pool to relive your confrontation with the Twisted Marionette. Destroy it, and reduce it once more to scrap metal and failed ambition.

Hero,For a time, the flow of the Mists supports your growth as one of Tyria's defenders. While this persists, your adventures will grant you further experience and richer rewards—take advantage of the opportunity to train and prepare yourself for the trials to come.I, too, will reinforce your path while I am able. Meet me in your homeland, and—should you prove yourself with a simple task—I will grant you a weapon to support your journey to the pinnacles of valor.—Mist Stranger

Hero,The Mists tremble and warp as the impact of recent events permeates the beyond. The Astral Ward has named it a fractal incursion, as Tyria risks a deluge of malicious attacks from fractal recreations.I am calling for your aid to stabilize the disturbance via the Fractals of the Mists in Lion's Arch. Let us meet there, and I will explain how we may avert this crisis.—Your Ally

Hey there, Explorer! The Priory has another job for us. A tribe of hylek has been sacrificing its greatest warriors to a dragon minion. They think they're appeasing the beast, but they're actually just giving it more things to make undead. We're going to show them they're only making the enemy stronger. I'm enclosing the hylek village's location. See you there!—Magister Sieran

Hey there, Explorer. The Priory wants us to look into reports of quaggans going missing over in Wyrmblood Lake. By all accounts, the dragons are involved, so that means we're involved. Besides, it's a place I've never been and people I haven't met, so I'm really looking forward to it. See you there!—Magister Sieran

Hey! %str1%! Get ready to be impressed! I finished the machine! I'm about to perform the final tests, and Kasmeer is here too trying to tell me something about Marjory and Lazarus or whatever. The important thing is I FINISHED THE MACHINE!—Taimi
Hey! Are you still with me?
Hey! Wake up! Oh, okay. Don't drift out like that.

Hey, %str1%,There have been whispers floating around the camp of your deeds. You've proven your movements are as soft as a shadow, as swift as a breeze, as smooth as a cub's belly fur, as sassy as a Blood Legionnaire's tail, as...well, I think you get the idea.Of course, I'll tell the rest of the soldiers you learned it from the best first.— Fina Duskmaker

Hey, Commander!Sorry for sending this antiquated note, but while I was working with Yao and Joon, communications became critically important to solving their power problems. The good news is, we made some serious upgrades to the comms systems I developed. The bad news is, I can't just patch the system. I have to roll this out to every user individually...AND you won't be on the upgraded network until you get the update. Hence, the mail. I've attached a one-time-use service chip that'll apply the update to your comms unit automatically. Once you perform the update, you won't need to do anything...but feel free to test it out. Everyone else should have theirs already...this tech is still experimental, so you may run into some glitches if you overextend the system.TaimiPS: Ivan's been calling me every five minutes, complaining that he has something important, yadda yadda yadda... Maybe ask him what he needs, so he stops bugging me while I'm trying to work? Okay, gotta go... BYE!
Hey, Commander, Thanks so much for your help keeping things in order at the Bazaar. It can get crazy around here—especially lately—and your assistance was invaluable.

Hey, Commander,I'm headed to the Eye of the North with Braham. For a while now, he's been working up the nerve to have a talk with Jormag. Not sure why he asked me to come along—hand-holding's more your thing. But I won't say no to a friend.Just so you know. In case you wanted to join.—Rytlock

Hey, Commander,Long time no see. Got a favor to ask. Since we're done dealing in demons (thank gods), I thought I'd give the whole Red Duck Teahouse get-together another go.Fine, I'll level with you. Truth is, I asked Min to dinner at the teahouse. But the closer it gets, the more I worry I'll screw the whole thing up.For as much BS as you all drag me into, I think having a few friends there would help things go a lot smoother. Gorrik and Taimi have already agreed to show. If you wouldn't mind swinging by for a bit, I'd appreciate it.Hopefully see you soon,Rama

Hey, friends,Enjoying the peace and quiet lately? Me neither. What in the world do people DO?If you've been missing my company lately (you know who you are!), meet me at the Red Duck Teahouse for food and drinks. I already ordered lots of sushi. And some good food, too.—RamaPS: Sorry for the generic invite, but there are a lot of you, and paperwork isn't my thing anymore—I'm retired.

Hey, good work! I'm awfully proud of you.You've proven that you can stand up and fight for yourself.You've also proven you can tend your own wounds, even in an unexpected situation, which tells me you're ready to face the challenges of combat.Now it is time for you to show the courage to walk away from my training grounds and into the unknown.Shadowy creatures have stolen the luminous orbs that Kella needs to light the island, and she needs them returned to her. I'm sure you can handle it. It'll be a good exercise for you!—SigneP.S. I've sent some money along with this letter. Don't forget to <c=@quest>take all</c> of the coins out of the envelope!
Hey, have you seen our commander?

Hey, old friend! From what I hear, you've been making quite the impression with the local townsfolk. Hearing how much you've progressed fills me with pride. I urge you to continue your good works in Queensdale and find more people in need. Do that, and you'll make a name for yourself in no time.—Kella

Hey, Slayer! I've got more trouble than I can handle on my caravan delivery routes, and Eir Stegalkin said I should talk to you. I need some serious muscle to help me get where I'm going, and I reckon the Slayer of Issormir has what it takes. Up for a challenge? Come see me, and I'll make it worth your while.—Ferghen the Tracker
Hey, thanks for cleaning out the pests in our training area. I haven't seen a single skritt since! I'll make sure the legions hear about your hard work.—Thegren Topjaw
Hey, you! Our egg gatherers haven't reported back yet. Go south and see if they need a hand.

Hi there! I hate writing letters, so I'll get straight to the point. You've been a great help at the skimmer ranch, and I think you've earned their trust enough to have a skimmer of your own.Come find me when you're free, and I'll sell you a skimmer saddle.—Ardra
Hi there! I'd love to chat, but right now we're dealing with a small-scale chak invasion. Gotta go!

Hi there...again! Look at me, writing another letter. First off, thanks again for helping out at my camp. I'm glad you're such a helpful person, because I need your help again now that you've got a skimmer to help you get around.I've attached a note to this letter. Take it to Ebele, please. It contains information on Forged and Awakened troop movements. We've used it to keep our food shipments safe. Hopefully, Ebele can use it for her exiles.We all need to survive out here, and we have common enemies. The more we can do to help each other, the stronger we'll all be.—Ardra

Hi! I felt it appropriate to thank you for everything you did. You're a true friend of the Soundless, and you'll always be welcome here.Life on the isle is still difficult, but we continue to persevere, thanks in no small part to all your help. Be sure to stop by Mabon Market, just east of the island. I'm sure there's a need for someone as talented as yourself.Thanks again!—Eona

Hi, hi, hi! I've got something fantabulous to show you. Get here as soon as you can. There's a gate set up in Rata Sum, but it's top secret. Fortunately, you know me.Go to Rata Sum and look for the Adventure Aide ZK496i golem. It knows to let you in.—Taimi
Hi,Cane stopped in to resupply and mentioned your name. You must have really been kicking tail—being noticed by him is high praise! Just thought you might want to know.—Raquel

Hi,I wanted to thank you for helping us salvage treasure out here. The undead cause trouble wherever they go, and having someone to help deal with them makes a huge difference.—Admiral Clarinda Demard
